content: For the three-bus power system shown in the figure, the trip signals to the circuit breakers B$$_1$$ to B$$_9$$ are provided by overcurrent relays R$$_1$$ to R$$_9$$, respectively, some of which have directional properties also. The necessary condition for the system to be protected for short circuit fault at any part of the system between bus 1 and the R-L loads with isolation of minimum portion of the network using minimum number of directional relays is

options.content: R$$_3$$ and R$$_4$$ are directional overcurrent relays blocking faults towards bus 2
R$$_3$$ and R$$_4$$ are directional overcurrent relays blocking faults towards bus 2 and R$$_7$$ is directional overcurrent relay blocking faults towards bus 3

R$$_3$$ and R$$_4$$ are directional overcurrent relays blocking faults towards Line 1 and Line 2, respectively, R$$_7$$ is directional overcurrent relay blocking faults towards Line 3 and R$$_5$$ is directional overcurrent relay blocking faults towards bus 2
R$$_3$$ and R$$_4$$ are directional overcurrent relays blocking faults towards Line 1 and Line 2, respectively.

explanation:
Hence, $R_3$ and $R_4$ are directional over current relays, which only operates when fault occurs in line - 1 and line - 2 respectively. But blocking faults towards bus - 2.

Hence, the correct option is (A).

A negative sequence relay is commonly used to protect

An $$'a'$$ phase to ground fault with zero fault impedance occurs at the centre of the transmission line. Bus voltage at $$X$$ and line current from $$X$$ to $$F$$ for the phase $$'a',$$ are given by $${V_a}$$ Volts and $${{\rm I}_a}$$ Amperes, respectively. Then, the impedance measured by the ground distance relay located at the terminal $$X$$ of line $$XY$$ will be given by
In a biased differential relay the bias is defined as a ratio of

options.content: Number of turns of restraining and operating coil
Operating coil current and restraining coil current
Fault current and operating coil current
Fault current and restraining coil current

Consider the problem of relay co-ordination for the distance relays $$R1$$ and $$R2$$ on adjacent lines of a transmission system figure. The Zone $$1$$
and Zone $$2$$ settings for both the relays are indicated on the diagram. Which of the following indicates the correct time setting for the Zone $$2$$ of relays $$R1$$ and $$R2.$$
In a 3-step distance protection, the reach of the three zones of the relay at the beginning of the first line typically extends upto

options.content: 100% of the first line, 50% of the second line and 20% of the third line.
80% of the first line, 50% of the second line and 20% of the third line.
80% of the first line, 20% of the second line and 10% of the third line.
50% of first line, 50% of second line and 20% of the third line.

content: A $$3$$-phase transformer rated for $$33 kV/11kV$$ is connected in delta/star as shown in figure. The relays are IDMT is natural having the characteristic

Voltage phasors at the two terminals of a transmission line of length $$70$$ km have  a magnitude of $$1.0$$ per unit but are $$180$$ degrees out of phase. Assuming that the maximum load current in the line is $$1/5$$<sup>th</sup> of minimum $$3$$-phase fault current. Which one of the following transmission line protection schemes will NOT pick up for this condition?

Directional over current protection set to pick up at $$1.25$$ times the maximum load current
Pilot relaying system with directional comparison scheme
Pilot relaying system with segregated phase comparison scheme.

content:

The per unit voltages of two synchronous machines connected through a lossless line are $$0.95\,\angle {10^ \circ }$$ and $$1.0\,\angle {0^ \circ }.$$ Match the two sides in the following
answer: $$\left( a \right) - \left( P \right),\,\,\left( b \right) - \left( Q \right),\,\,\left( c \right) - \left( T \right)$$

content: The distance relay with inherent directional property is known as ________relay.

Type of Relay
$$A.$$ Buchholz Relay
$$B.$$ Translay relay
$$C.$$ Carrier current, phase comparison relay
$$D.$$ Directional over Current relay
$$E.$$ Negative sequence relay

Most suited for
$$P.$$ Feeder
$$Q.$$ Transformer
$$R.$$ Radial distributed
$$S.$$ Generator
$$T.$$ Ring main distributor
$$U.$$ Long over head transmission line
answer: $$\left( A \right) - \left( B \right),\,\,\left( B \right) - \left( P \right),\,\,\left( C \right) - \left( U \right),\,\,\left( D \right) - \left( T \right),\,\,\left( E \right) - \left( S \right)$$
